symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive change in bowel habit with thin, pencil-like stool calibre and incomplete emptying

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New persistent altered bowel habit and stool calibre in an older adult is a recognised lower-GI alarm feature for a distal colonic/rectal lesion.
Luminal narrowing of any cause can alter calibre; non-specific in isolation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Melaena and intermittent bright red rectal bleeding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Persistent GI blood loss from a friable luminal tumour is a hallmark alarm feature and the source of the anaemia.
Melaena and significant PR bleeding are not features of simple constipation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional ~10 kg weight loss with profound fatigue

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unexplained constitutional weight loss with fatigue is a high-yield malignancy alarm feature (tumour cachexia + anaemia).

symptoms.symptom_name: Escalating wave-like colicky abdominal pain with distension and bloating

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Crescendo colicky pain with distension is the cardinal symptom of mechanical bowel obstruction and defines the acute complication driving the tier.
Obstruction here is the presenting complication of the underlying obstructing tumour.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tenesmus / persistent sensation of incomplete rectal emptying

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tenesmus suggests a distal rectosigmoid mass occupying the rectum.

symptoms.symptom_name: Conjunctival pallor indicating probable significant anaemia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective pallor on clinician exam supports significant anaemia from chronic GI blood loss — a clinician observation, not patient-obtainable; the patient/carer only report looking 'pale'.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distended abdomen with high-pitched 'tinkling' bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distension with hyperactive tinkling bowel sounds is a classic obstructive exam finding — auscultation is impossible via telehealth, so sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable firm, fixed mass in the left lower quadrant

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A fixed palpable colonic mass strongly indicates neoplasm; requires clinician palpation, unavailable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dark blood on digital rectal examination; no rigid peritonism on exam

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: DRE confirming dark blood and the absence of peritonism are clinician exam findings that cannot be obtained remotely; sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Full blood count / iron studies (expected iron-deficiency anaemia)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood count and iron studies
investigation_details.expected_result: microcytic hypochromic anaemia with low ferritin consistent with chronic GI blood loss
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Anaemia severity and transfusion need unquantified; but bloods require venepuncture unavailable in telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the anaemia clinically suspected from pallor and fatigue; hospital/pathology-obtained, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: CT abdomen/pelvis and colonoscopy with biopsy

investigation_details.expected_result: obstructing colonic mass with proximal dilatation on CT; friable luminal tumour on colonoscopy confirmed on histology
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Obstruction level, completeness and tissue diagnosis remain undefined; these are the definitive confirmatory tests and are hospital-only.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Colorectal carcinoma with large bowel obstruction
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging plus histology are the confirmatory standard; entirely outside telehealth capability, hence 'cannot_diagnose_remotely'.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Change in bowel habit + rectal bleeding/melaena + unintentional weight loss + tenesmus in an older adult is the classic 2-week-wait alarm cluster mandating urgent lower-GI cancer investigation.
Crescendo colicky pain, distension and tinkling bowel sounds define the acute mechanical obstruction — the complication that sets the urgent (T4) tier and the perforation escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Maps the obstruction and the mass; the correct next step for suspected large bowel obstruction, arranged through same-day ED assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, iron studies, urea/electrolytes/creatinine, lactate (via ED)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies anaemia, hydration/renal status and ischaemia risk; requires venepuncture, hence hospital-obtained.
Definitive tissue diagnosis and staging pathway once the acute obstruction is managed; not a telehealth or immediate-acute investigation.
Contraindicated in suspected mechanical obstruction — risks raising intraluminal pressure and precipitating perforation; the AI must NOT recommend this.
